example: You need to **get ready for the test** by reviewing all the chapters.
Please **review your notes** before the class starts tomorrow.
If you want to pass the exam, you must **study hard** every day.
**Good luck on the test**! I know you will do well.
Don't worry, **you will do great** on the presentation.
You should **trust yourself** and your abilities.
Always **be confident** when you speak in public.
Please **stay calm** during the emergency drill.
**Don't be nervous** about the interview, just be yourself.
**Take a deep breath** before you start speaking.
You must **read carefully** to understand the instructions.
It is important to **think before answering** difficult questions.
Always **check your work** before submitting it.
You need to **use your time wisely** to finish all tasks.
**Don't rush** when you are doing important work.
Students should learn to **manage their time** effectively.
You can **skip difficult questions** and answer them later.
If you are stuck, **come back later** with a fresh mind.
Please make sure to **answer all questions** on the form.
You must **write clearly** so others can read your handwriting.
The teacher asked students to **write neatly** in their notebooks.
Please **use pen** to sign the document.
You can **use pencil** to draw the sketch.
The teacher reminded everyone that **no cheating** is allowed.
You should always **be honest** with your friends.
Students must **do their own work** to learn effectively.
**Don't copy others**' work, it is not right.
Please **focus on your paper** during the exam.
**Keep your eyes on** your own desk.
The rule is **no talking during the test**.
**Silence please** while the exam is in progress.
When the bell rings, you must **stop writing** immediately.
Everyone, **put down your pens** now.
Please **hand in your papers** to the teacher.
**The test is over** and students can leave now.
**How did you do** on the math exam?
**Are you confident** about your answers?
**Did you finish** all the questions on time?
**Was it easy** or difficult for you?
**Was it difficult** to solve the problem?
**What did you learn** from this experience?
**What did you study** in school today?
**How can you improve** your English skills?
You should **set a goal** for this semester.
Let's **make a plan** for the project together.
Don't just dream, **take action** to achieve your goals.
You can **track your progress** using this app.
We should **celebrate success** after winning the game.
It is important to **learn from failure** and try again.
No matter how hard it is, **never give up** on your dreams.
